import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from IPython.display import Image, display
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

Electrification Inequality and Spatial Infrastructure: A Tile-Level Panel Analysis of Brazil, China, and Morocco (2014–2023)¶

Authors: Namibarsbold · Amanda · Bouchra
Repository: https://github.com/namibarsbold/STATS201-Course-project
GitHub Pages: https://namibarsbold.github.io/STATS201-Course-project/

1. Research Question and Motivation¶

⟶ Team: replace this placeholder with content ⟵

  • What is the social science question or problem?
  • Why does it matter?
  • What would success look like?

2. Data¶

2.1 Sources¶

⟶ Team: describe data sources here ⟵

  • VIIRS Nighttime Lights: NOAA/VIIRS/DNB/MONTHLY_V1/VCMSLCFG via Google Earth Engine (annual composites, 500 m resolution, 2014–2023)
  • WorldPop population rasters (1 km, annual)
  • OpenStreetMap infrastructure data (roads, power lines) via Geofabrik

2.2 Unit of Analysis¶

⟶ Team: describe the unit of analysis ⟵

The unit of analysis is a spatial tile (approximately 0.5° × 0.5°) within each country, observed annually from 2014 to 2023. Each tile constitutes a country-tile-year observation.

2.3 Key Variables¶

⟶ Team: describe key variables ⟵

Variable Role Description
mean_rad Target (label) Mean nightly radiance (nW/cm²/sr) per tile per year
log_pop Feature Log of WorldPop population estimate
road_density Feature Road length per km² (OSM)
power_density Feature Power line length per km² (OSM)
country Group variable Brazil, China, Morocco
year Time variable 2014–2023

2.4 Preprocessing¶

⟶ Team: describe preprocessing steps ⟵

In [2]:
# EDA: Distribution of nighttime light radiance
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4))
axes[0].imshow(plt.imread('../figures/fig_hist_mean_rad.png'))
axes[0].axis('off')
axes[0].set_title('Figure 1a — Distribution of Mean Radiance', fontsize=10)
axes[1].imshow(plt.imread('../figures/fig_scatter_mean_vs_sd.png'))
axes[1].axis('off')
axes[1].set_title('Figure 1b — Mean vs SD of Radiance', fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

2.5 Ethical Considerations¶

⟶ Team: discuss ethical considerations ⟵

All data used in this study are publicly available at the tile/raster level and do not contain personally identifiable information. VIIRS nighttime light data are aggregate satellite observations; WorldPop rasters are modeled population estimates. No individual-level data are used. Cross-country comparisons should be interpreted cautiously given differences in administrative structure, reporting standards, and economic contexts.

3. Problem Setup¶

⟶ Team: describe the ML problem setup ⟵

  • ML task: Regression — predicting the level (or change) of nighttime light radiance per tile
  • Target variable: Mean annual radiance (mean_rad) per tile
  • Inputs/features: Population density, road density, power-line density, urban/rural classification, country fixed effects, year
  • Key assumption: Nighttime light radiance is a valid proxy for electricity access and economic activity at the tile level

In [5]:
# R² comparison across models and countries
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.imshow(plt.imread('../figures/final report/fig1_r2_comparison.png'))
ax.axis('off')
ax.set_title('Figure 3 — R² Comparison: Baseline vs Final Model', fontsize=11)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [6]:
# Scatter plots: predicted vs actual (2023) for each country
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4.5))
for ax, country in zip(axes, ['Brazil', 'China', 'Morocco']):
    ax.imshow(plt.imread(f'../figures/week7_outputs/scatter_{country}_2023.png'))
    ax.axis('off')
    ax.set_title(f'Figure 4{chr(96 + list(["Brazil","China","Morocco"]).index(country) + 1)} — {country} (2023)', fontsize=10)
plt.suptitle('Predicted vs Actual Nighttime Light — 2023', fontsize=12, y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

6. Error Analysis¶

⟶ Team: describe where and why the model fails ⟵

# Residual diagnostics
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4.5))
axes[0].imshow(plt.imread('../figures/final report/fig5_residuals.png'))
axes[0].axis('off')
axes[0].set_title('Figure 5a — Residual Distribution', fontsize=10)
axes[1].imshow(plt.imread('../figures/final report/figure_residual_maps_Morocco_2020.png'))
axes[1].axis('off')
axes[1].set_title('Figure 5b — Residual Map: Morocco (2020)', fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

axes[0].imshow(plt.imread('../figures/final report/fig3_elasticity_comparison.png'))

axes[1].axis('off')
axes[1].set_title('Figure 6b — Temporal R² Stability (2014–2023)', fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
